headline: Anthropic × Blackstone / Hellman & Friedman / Goldman Sachs — $1.5B enterprise AI services JV (today):
summary: Anthropic launched a new standalone AI services firm to embed Claude into the core operations of portfolio companies, particularly private-equity-owned businesses in healthcare, manufacturing, financial services, retail, and real estate.

headline: OpenAI — "The Deployment Company" $10B JV (today):
summary: OpenAI finalised a $10B joint venture anchored by TPG, with 19 total investors including Brookfield Asset Management, Advent, and Bain Capital.

headline: Google — Gemini Embedding 2 generally available:
summary: Google's first natively multimodal embedding model moved from public preview (March 10) to GA via the Gemini API and Vertex AI.

headline: Mistral — Medium 3.5 + Vibe remote agents (May 2):
summary: Alongside the Medium 3.5 128B model weights (see Models section), Mistral shipped "Mistral Vibe CLI," a cloud-based remote coding agent that opens pull requests autonomously, and a new "Work Mode" in Le Chat that orchestrates multi-step autonomous tasks — email triage, research synthesis, and cross-

headline: DeepSeek-V4-Pro
summary: (`deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V4-Pro`, MIT, trending #1 — 534.9K downloads, 3,520 likes): 1.6 trillion total parameters, 49B activated.

headline: Xiaomi MiMo-V2.5-Pro
summary: (`XiaomiMiMo/MiMo-V2.5-Pro`, MIT, trending #2 — 11.8K downloads, 423 likes): 1.02 trillion-parameter MoE with 42B active parameters.

headline: Mistral Medium 3.5 128B
summary: (`mistralai/Mistral-Medium-3.5-128B`, proprietary commercial licence, trending #4 — 11.9K downloads, 253 likes): Dense 128B model, 256k context window; handles instruction-following, reasoning, and coding in a single set of weights.

headline: Poolside Laguna XS.2
summary: (`poolside/Laguna-XS.2`, Apache 2.0, trending #7 — 10.4K downloads, 208 likes): 33B total / 3B active parameters (MoE); designed for agentic coding on local hardware — fits on a single consumer GPU or an Apple Silicon Mac with 36 GB RAM via Ollama.

headline: SulphurAI Sulphur-2-base
summary: (`SulphurAI/Sulphur-2-base`, Apache 2.0, trending #10 — 20.2K downloads, 164 likes): Open image-generation (diffusers/GGUF) model released May 3; last modified today (2026-05-04T06:18Z).

headline: OpenAI privacy-filter
summary: (`openai/privacy-filter`, Apache 2.0, trending #3 — 132.6K downloads, 1,254 likes): Token-classification NER model for PII detection; ONNX format with Transformers.js support for browser/edge deployment.

headline: inclusionAI Ling-2.6-flash
summary: (`inclusionAI/Ling-2.6-flash`, MIT, trending #20 — 1.1K downloads, 455 likes): Ant Group's fast-inference agent model; 104B total / 7.4B active MoE, 260k context window.

headline: NIST CAISI evaluation of DeepSeek V4 Pro (May 2026):
summary: NIST's Center for AI Standards and Innovation published an independent capability assessment of DeepSeek V4 Pro — the first major US government evaluation of a Chinese open-weight frontier model.

headline: DeepSeek V4 Pro — coding benchmark detail:
summary: LiveCodeBench 93.5 (methodology: competitive-programming problems from Codeforces/LeetCode/AtCoder, monthly-refreshed to limit contamination); Codeforces ELO 3206 vs GPT-5.5 at 3168; SWE-bench Verified 80.6% vs Claude Opus 4.7 at 80.8% — statistically tied.

headline: Mistral Medium 3.5 — SWE-bench Verified 77.6%:
summary: Mistral reports 77.6% on SWE-bench Verified and 91.4% on τ³-Telecom (a domain-specific agentic tool-use benchmark in the telecommunications vertical).

headline: Xiaomi MiMo-V2.5-Pro — SWE-bench Pro 57.2%:
summary: 57.2% on SWE-bench Pro (a harder variant of SWE-bench with more complex, real-world GitHub issues); 64% Pass³ on ClawEval (multi-step agent evaluation, Chinese-origin benchmark); 40–60% fewer tokens than Claude Opus 4.6 at comparable task-completion rates per Xiaomi's internal evals.

headline: Poolside Laguna XS.2 — full benchmark profile:
summary: 68.2% SWE-bench Verified; 62.4% SWE-bench Multilingual; 44.5% SWE-bench Pro; 30.1% Terminal-Bench 2.0.

headline: Leaderboard state (no major shuffle today):
summary: LMArena text-generation currently places Opus 4.7 (thinking) and Claude Opus 4.6 (thinking) at the top of user-preference Elo rankings from anonymous A/B crowd voting.

headline: Competing enterprise AI JVs — the structural story of the day:
summary: The simultaneous announcements from Anthropic and OpenAI represent a deliberate strategic move by both labs away from SaaS API licensing as the primary revenue model and toward managed deployment services with embedded engineers.

headline: Parallel Web Systems raises $100M at $2B valuation (Sequoia):
summary: Parag Agrawal's post-Twitter AI company secured $100M led by Sequoia, targeting AI agent search and research use cases.

headline: Legora $50M extension — Nvidia and Atlassian back legal AI:
summary: Legal workflow AI startup Legora received a $50M extension with Nvidia and Atlassian as lead backers, reinforcing the regulated-industry vertical thesis.

headline: Q1 2026 venture funding — $300B all-time record:
summary: Crunchbase reports Q1 2026 set a global VC record at $300B, primarily driven by AI infrastructure and foundation-model rounds.

headline: US federal AI procurement — GSA Multiple Award Schedule Refresh 31:
summary: The General Services Administration introduced the first AI-specific acquisition regulation clause through MAS Refresh 31, covering: intellectual property and licensing terms, restrictions on government data (including derivative data, metadata, and logs — all owned by the government), data handling

headline: Ollama MLX backend — M5 acceleration (preview since March 31, ongoing):
summary: Ollama 0.19's shift from the llama.cpp Metal path to Apple's MLX framework as the default Apple Silicon inference backend remains the dominant local-inference story.

headline: Poolside Laguna XS.2 — practical local coding agent for Apple Silicon:
summary: Laguna XS.2's 33B-total / 3B-active MoE architecture makes it runnable on a Mac with 36 GB RAM via Ollama (M2 Ultra, M3 Ultra, or M4 Max).
